item.title: ShimBad the Sailor, Part 3
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: Windows 11 brings us a lot of new Shim-related goodies and it makes sense to cover at least some of them. In the second part of this series I listed a number of process names that are treated in a … Continue reading →

item.title: 1 little known secret of sti_ci.dll
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: In 2017 I posted about sideloading of sti_ci.dll. And it’s that DLL itself that executes the InstallWiaDevice installation command mentioned in that post… How? Via its export function called… InstallWiaDevice. It turns out that we can launch this API directly … Continue reading →

item.title: WinHttpOpen user agents
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: When you call WinHttpOpen API you can specify the user agent that will be used in subsequent WinHTTP calls. Windows OS and its native binaries use WinHttp APIs a lot, so the below is list of all user agents I … Continue reading →

item.title: 1 little known secret of CompatTelRunner.exe
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: When you run: the program will… sleep for exactly 1 second. The whatever bit can be any string, as long as it’s at least 1 character long.

item.title: 1 little known secret of net.exe
item.dc:creator: adam
item.description: In Windows 11 24H2+ and Windows Server 2025+ you can use new net.exe command line arguments to Configure alternative SMB ports: TCP, QUIC, RDMA: I am not the first one to point it out — see this article.
